title: strip_control_chars silently strips tab characters from messages
description: strip_control_chars preserves only \n among C0 controls; \t (0x09, HTAB) is stripped because is_control() returns true for it. The doc comment says 'preserving newlines' but does not mention tabs. A user who pastes tab-indented code into a message or receives a message with tab-separated columns will see the tabs silently deleted with no indication. A future contributor adding a \r whitelist may not realize \t is also stripped. — Maintenance consequence: silent data loss in message display; contributor adding \r support may not audit the full whitelist.
